title: inflation expectations / household survey / de anchoring | Research Paper | Why Have Inflation Expectations Surged Recently? A Historica...
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston researchers Philippe Andrade and Michael Wicklein analyze household inflation expectations using University of Michigan Survey of Consumers data and a regression model linking expectations to salient prices (gasoline, food) and core CPI. They find that the March 2025 surge in inflation expectations—like the late-1970s surge—cannot be fully explained by actual price increases, unlike the early 1970s and pandemic-era spikes. This unexplained component suggests increased risk of de-anchoring of inflation expectations, which could fuel additional price pressures and complicate the Fed's path to its 2 percent inflation target.
